items.title: Min-heap (tree view and array view)
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB R((1)) --> A((3)) R --> B((2)) A --> C((5)) A --> D((4)) B --> E((9)) B --> F((8)) Note["Array layout:
idx: 0 1 2 3 4 5 6
val: 1 3 2 5 4 9 8
parent(i)=(i-1)//2
left=2i+1, right=2i+2"]
items.caption: The tree is just a way to visualize the array; no pointers stored. Root is the minimum.

items.title: Sift-up after push
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Before["insert 0 at end (idx 7)"]
    direction TB
    B1((1)) --> B2((3))
    B1 --> B3((2))
    B2 --> B4((5))
    B2 --> B5((4))
    B3 --> B6((9))
    B3 --> B7((8))
    B4 --> B8((0))
  end
  subgraph After["sift up: 0 swaps with 5, then 3, then 1"]
    direction TB
    A1((0)) --> A2((1))
    A1 --> A3((2))
    A2 --> A4((3))
    A2 --> A5((4))
    A3 --> A6((9))
    A3 --> A7((8))
    A4 --> A8((5))
  end
  Before --> After
items.caption: Push appends to the end then walks up swapping with the parent while smaller — at most log n swaps.
